issues.title: Decision-gate strip advertises `[g] resume`, but `g` is not a board key
issues.description: In model.go gateFor() the default (waiting-for-user / decision gate) branch sets answer="[g] resume", which renderNeedsYouStrip prints as the gate row's answer affordance (`[N] read state · [g] resume`). But updateBoard (update.go) has no `g` case — `case "g"` only exists in updateViewer (viewport GotoTop). The actual resume/move key for a waiting-for-user card (ClassInProgress) is `m` (move.go: "in-progress → go(resume)"). So a decision-gate row tells the user to press `g`, which is a no-op on the board. Only the awaiting-uat and awaiting-plan-acceptance gates (answer `[d] ship` / `[m] accept`, both real board keys) are correct. This path is real (mid-UAT re-plan / parked decision) but has no fixture, so tests didn't catch it. The number-key jump still works (it focuses the card + opens the drill), so only the secondary answer-key label is wrong.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE: In model.go gateFor()'s default (decision) branch change answer from "[g] resume" to "[m] resume" (matching move.go's in-progress → go/resume via `m`). Optionally add a fixture with a waiting-for-user feature so the decision-gate row is asserted like the uat gate is in TestNeedsYouStripRender.

issues.title: Dead leftovers from the badge→pill migration (`uatStyle`, `colStyleSet.badge`)
issues.description: The implement round removed cardBadgeText/badgeStyleFor (superseded by pillLabel/pillStyleFor) but left their two collaborators orphaned: styles.go:107 `uatStyle` and the `badge` field of colStyleSet (declared in the struct, still assigned in init() at styles.go:121). Confirmed via grep that neither is read anywhere in cli/ anymore — `badgeStyleFor(f, columnStyles[colIdx].badge)` in the old view.go was their sole consumer (verified against HEAD). Go does not flag unused package vars / struct fields, so gofmt+vet stay green, but this is dead code that the coding-rules "no dead or mocked-out code" / "keep diffs minimal" bar asks to remove.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE: delete `uatStyle` from styles.go and remove the `badge lipgloss.Style` field from colStyleSet plus its assignment in init(). (uatAccent itself stays — it is still used by pillPurple and stripeAccent.)

issues.title: gateNumberKey parses only 1–9; strip advertises `[N]` for N that can exceed 9
issues.description: gateNumberKey (update.go) accepts a single digit 1..9 (`len(s)==1 && '1'..'9'`). renderNeedsYouStrip labels each gate row `[%d]` = i+1 and the degraded summary says `press 1–%d to answer`, both of which can render `[10]`, `[11]`, … when more than nine cards are simultaneously WaitingForInput(). Those gates are then unreachable by number key (`10` is len 2 → rejected). Practical impact is very low (>9 concurrent user gates is unusual, and the strip would already be very tall / degraded), but the advertised affordance and the handler disagree.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE (optional): cap the per-row number affordance to the first 9 gates (show `[1]`..`[9]` and drop or plain-list the rest), or keep as-is and treat as wontfix given the negligible real-world frequency. No behavioural change is required to advance.
